title: Transitional Housing/Shelter * Homeless Families - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Housing & Shelter > Transitional Housing/Shelter * Homeless Families 13 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - Homeless Youth nsitional Housing/Shelter AIDS/HIV Crime Victims Disabilities Issues Homeless Families Homeless Men Homeless Veterans Homeless Women Pregnant Individuals Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF AGAPE HOUSE MARTHA'S HOUSE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides transitional housing to families and women escaping domestic violence or experiencing homelessness. Also offers supportive services including social service referrals, job readiness, budgeting, and other life skills in preparation for independent living. Eligibility: Families with children ages 0-18, or single women. Must be alcohol and drug free, and no pets. Hours: House: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Office: Tuesday-Thursday 9am-noon and 1pm-3pm, Friday 9am-noon Email (541) 571-3963 Visit Website More Details BETHLEHEM INN FAMILIES FIRST PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides short-term shelter stays for an average of 5 weeks. Services available to residents include meals, case management services, laundry facilities, access to personal hygiene and clothing, and referrals to outside community resources. Children will be allowed to maintain enrollment at their current school providing it is within the service area. 20363 Mervin Sampels Road Bend, OR 97703 Eligibility: Families with children under age 18 who meet all of the following requirements: -Must pass drug test (drug/alcohol testing during intake and at random) -Must not have a registered sex offense -School-aged children must be enrolled in school While pets are not allowed, reasonable accommodations will be made for service animals. Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week; must check in by 5:30pm Email (541) 322-8768 13 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CHURCH @ THE PARK CCS MICRO SHELTER FOR FAMILIES CCS MICRO SHELTER FOR FAMILIES WITH CHILDREN Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides transitional housing with access to case management services. Clients must have the ability to manage their own activities of independent daily living. This program operates on a waitlist. Eligibility: Any combination of adults caring for children under the age of 18 Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (971) 375-7491 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FAMILY PROMISE OF TUALATIN VALLEY FAMILY SHELTER PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers long-term transitional housing with case management and specialized information and referral resources to assist clients in finding sustainable housing. Does not provide emergency or short-term shelter. 11460 SW Pacific Highway Portland, OR 97223 Eligibility: Homeless families and pregnant women currently experiencing homelessness or within 14 days of homelessness occurring Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (503) 640-3263 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HELPING HANDS REENTRY OUTREACH CENTERS HELPING HANDS REENTRY BYBEE LAKES HOPE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides emergency shelter and transitional housing. Participants have access to food and clothing, assistance with applying for health insurance, and a comprehensive needs assessment process supported by a case manager. Can provide information and referrals about identification replacement, schooling, and job placement services. 14355 N Bybee Lake Court Portland, OR 97203 Eligibility: -Adults age 18 and older and families with children under age 18 experiencing homelessness or are re-entering the community from incarceration -Clients must not have or be under the influence of any intoxicants on-site. Individuals with a registered sex offense are not eligible. Hours: Phone: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Emergency Shelter: 24 hours per day / 7 days a week Email (971) 333-5070 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HELPING HANDS REENTRY OUTREACH CENTERS HELPING HANDS REENTRY CLATSOP COUNTY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides emergency shelter and transitional housing. Participants have access to food and clothing, assistance with applying for health insurance, and a comprehensive needs assessment process supported by a case manager. Can provide information and referrals about identification replacement, schooling, and job placement services. 286 W Marine Drive Astoria, OR 97103 Eligibility: -Adults age 18 and older and families with children under age 18 experiencing homelessness or are re-entering the community from incarceration -Clients must not have or be under the influence of any intoxicants on-site. Individuals with a registered sex offense are not eligible. Hours: Phone: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Emergency Shelter: 24 hours per day / 7 days a week Email (503) 739-4347 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HELPING HANDS REENTRY OUTREACH CENTERS HELPING HANDS REENTRY TILLAMOOK COUNTY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides emergency shelter and transitional housing. Participants have access to food and clothing, assistance with applying for health insurance, and a comprehensive needs assessment process supported by a case manager. Can provide information and referrals about identification replacement, schooling, and job placement services. 6505 Headquarter Street Tillamook, OR 97141 Eligibility: -Adults age 18 and older and families with children under age 18 experiencing homelessness or are re-entering the community from incarceration -Clients must not have or be under the influence of any intoxicants on-site. Individuals with a registered sex offense are not eligible. Hours: Phone: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Emergency Shelter: 24 hours per day / 7 days a week Email (503) 354-8014 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MID-COLUMBIA COMMUNITY ACTION COUNCIL THE ANNEX TRANSITIONAL HOUSING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a transitional living shelter with access to case management services to assist clients in overcoming barriers to stable housing. 200 W 2nd Street The Dalles, OR 97058 Eligibility: Adults age 18 and older and families with minor children who are experiencing homelessness. Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 980-0855 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MY FATHER'S HOUSE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides transitional housing where participants have access to parenting and life skills classes, the Rent Well tenant education program, and support from a case manager. A background check and urinalysis is required. Eligibility: Homeless families with minor children, meet income guidelines, be clean and sober, willing to actively seek full-time employment Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (503) 492-3046 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PARKER HOUSE PROJECT PARKER HOUSE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a transitional housing program with individualized case management, support, and mentoring services. 2895 Highway 66 Ashland, OR 97520 Eligibility: Women age 18 and older and women with minor children Hours: Information/Intake: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm House: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week for current residents Email (541) 500-7381 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ROGUE RETREAT REDWOOD INN Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers transitional shelter. Features 21 medical respite spaces specifically tailored to those needing safe and sanitary environments to heal. Also provides 23 studio apartments dedicated to individuals and families in a transitional housing program. 711 E Main Street Suite 25 Medford, OR 97504 Eligibility: Individuals and couples age 18 and older, and families with minor children Hours: Phone: Monday-Friday 8am-noon and 1pm-5pm Shelter: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 499-0880 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SAMARITAN HOUSE RESIDENCY PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a 10-month transitional living program where residents can establish rental history, receive guidance on education and employment, and access limited child care. Residents are required to deposit at least 60% of their monthly income into a savings account through the organization, which is then used to cover move-in costs when they transition to a new residence. 715 SW Bay Street Newport, OR 97365 Eligibility: Pregnant women within 14 days of birth or homeless families with dependent children under the age of 18. Parents must have custody of their children at least 50% of the time. Residents must not use drugs or alcohol while completing the program. Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm (541) 574-8898 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ST FRANCIS FAMILY HOUSING TRANSITIONAL HOUSING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides transitional housing services coupled with life-skills support and wraparound services to assist in attaining self-sufficiency. Length of stay may range from 3-9 months. Eligibility: Homeless families with children under the age of 18 Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-4pm Email (503) 588-0428 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BETHLEHEM INN FAMILIES FIRST PROGRAM FAMILY PROMISE OF TUALATIN VALLEY FAMILY SHELTER PROGRAM HELPING HANDS REENTRY OUTREACH CENTERS HELPING HANDS REENTRY BYBEE LAKES HOPE CENTER HELPING HANDS REENTRY OUTREACH CENTERS HELPING HANDS REENTRY CLATSOP COUNTY HELPING HANDS REENTRY OUTREACH CENTERS HELPING HANDS REENTRY TILLAMOOK COUNTY MID-COLUMBIA COMMUNITY ACTION COUNCIL TRANSITIONAL HOUSING PARKER HOUSE PROJECT PARKER HOUSE ROGUE RETREAT REDWOOD INN SAMARITAN HOUSE RESIDENCY PROGRAM
